title: Soya Keema

subs.name: minced mushrooms (250 g) — not as high in protein but same dry-mince texture
diced carrot or corn — adds crunch and colour
reduce to 1 tsp and use a non-stick pan — lower calorie version

steps.text: Soak the soya granules in 300 ml hot water with a pinch of salt for 10 minutes until they swell and become tender. Drain through a fine strainer and squeeze firmly with your hands to remove all moisture — the drier they are, the better they will absorb the masala without making it watery.
Heat oil in a kadai on medium. Add cumin seeds and let them sputter for 10 seconds. Add onion and sauté for 5-6 minutes until golden brown — this forms the flavour base, so do not rush it.

Stir in ginger-garlic paste and green chilli, fry for 1 minute until fragrant. Add tomatoes, season with a pinch of salt and cook on medium-high for 5 minutes, pressing the tomatoes until they break down and the oil separates around the edges.

Lower the flame and add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder; stir for 30 seconds. Add the squeezed soya granules and peas, increase flame to medium and stir-fry everything together for 5-6 minutes until the mixture looks dry and each granule is coated in spiced masala.

Sprinkle garam masala and a squeeze of lemon, taste for salt and finish with coriander leaves. Serve hot as a stuffing for parathas, alongside warm rotis, or packed as a lunchbox protein with steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: soya-chunks → minced mushrooms (250 g) — not as high in protein but same dry-mince texture; peas → diced carrot or corn — adds crunch and colour; oil → reduce to 1 tsp and use a non-stick pan — lower calorie version
